senses.definition: an immature larva that develops just before the pupa stage in certain insects
examples: The caterpillar enters the prepupa stage before forming a chrysalis.
Scientists studied the prepupa to understand metamorphosis in butterflies.

senses.definition: plural of prepupa; multiple immature insects in the prepupa stage
examples: The prepupae were examined under a microscope.
During the summer, several prepupae were observed in the insect colony.

senses.definition: relating to or occurring just before the pupal stage in insects
examples: The prepupal molt marks the final transition before pupation.
Scientists studied prepupal behavior in the life cycle.

senses.definition: plural of prepupa; immature insects before the pupal stage
examples: Researchers counted several prepupas in the tray.
The life cycle notes mention the prepupas briefly.

senses.definition: the act of buying something before it is available or before a formal purchase; an advance purchase
examples: There was a prepurchase agreement for the software.
They made a prepurchase deposit to secure the product.

senses.definition: relating to or occurring before a purchase
examples: Prepurchase surveys help gauge customer interest.
They discussed prepurchase considerations.

senses.definition: bought beforehand; purchased in advance
examples: The item was prepurchased to secure the discount.
Customers prepurchased tickets before the event.

senses.definition: a person who buys something in advance or before it is available
examples: The prepurchaser secured early access to the launch.
Only prepurchasers could enter the beta program.

senses.definition: purchases made in advance or before a product is released
examples: The company reported several prepurchases of the new device.
Prepurchases helped forecast demand.

senses.definition: the act of buying something in advance; the process of preordering
examples: Prepurchasing is common for limited-edition releases.
They discussed prepurchasing policies.

senses.definition: the intention or aim that precedes or comes before a primary purpose
examples: The prepurpose of the plan was to gather data.
They clarified the prepurpose before starting the project.

senses.definition: having been determined or intended beforehand
examples: The prepurposed changes were explained in the meeting.
A prepurposed plan guided the early steps.

senses.definition: the act or process of intending or planning beforehand
examples: Prepurposing guided the research design.
Prepurposing helped align resources.

senses.definition: having an intent or aim determined beforehand; purposeful in advance
examples: The prepurposive approach shaped the project from the start.
They adopted a prepurposive framework.

senses.definition: relating to or situated near or on the prepuce (the foreskin)
examples: Preputial skin covers the glans penis.
Medical exams sometimes involve inspecting preputial tissue.

senses.definition: an anatomical term for the fold of skin surrounding the glans; the foreskin
examples: The preputium is part of the external genitalia in males.
Anatomy texts describe the preputium and its functions.

senses.definition: situated near the pylorus, the opening from the stomach to the duodenum
examples: Prepyloric ulcers occur near the stomach outlet.
The prepyloric region was examined in the gastroscopy report.

senses.definition: relating to or shaped like a pyramid before the main structure; pre-pyramidal
examples: The prepyramidal arrangement formed the base of the sculpture.
Prepyramidal motifs appeared in early architectural plans.

senses.definition: the process of showing you have the necessary skills or money to do something or to buy something
examples: The bank requires prequalification before loan discussions.
Prequalification helps buyers understand if they are eligible.

senses.definition: plural of prequalification; multiple evidences of eligibility obtained in advance
examples: Applicants submitted their prequalifications before bidding.
Prequalifications are reviewed by the committee.

senses.definition: found to meet the necessary requirements in advance
examples: The bidders were prequalified for the contract.
Only prequalified buyers could access the auction.

senses.definition: forms of allow to be considered eligible before a process begins
examples: The vendor prequalifies candidates for the tender.
The program prequalifies applicants automatically.

senses.definition: to determine in advance that someone or something meets the necessary requirements
examples: They will prequalify borrowers before approving loans.
Prequalify your vehicle for the trade-in program.

senses.definition: the act of determining eligibility in advance; the process of prequalifying
examples: Prequalifying occurs before bids are opened.
The team discussed prequalifying criteria.

senses.definition: a period of quarantine before a potential exposure to prevent spread
examples: Farmers used a prequarantine period for new animals.
Prequarantine helps control disease introduction.

senses.definition: to subject to a quarantine period before a potential exposure
examples: They decided to prequarantine new livestock before entry.
The facility prequarantined visitors arriving from risk areas.

senses.definition: having undergone a quarantine period in advance
examples: Prequarantined animals were cleared for transport.
All prequarantined staff followed the protocol.
